title: podman pull unexpectly wastes lot of space for image
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I fail to pull 2.1GB container into VM that has 15GBB of free disk space. Container compressed size is 689.14 MB. VM runs out of diskspace.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. I use Fedora IoT with podman 1.8.2 https://iot.fedoraproject.org/ with 20GB of storage.

2. I pull this image as non-root user:
  ```docker pull ansible/awx_task:10.0.0```

3. It takes long, and eventually all disk space from root is gone (15GB)

**Describe the results you received:**

```
[ikke@fediot ~]$ podman pull ansible/awx_task:10.0.0                                                       
Trying to pull docker.io/ansible/awx_task:10.0.0...                                                        
Getting image source signatures                                                                            
Copying blob 2ff1d7ea1ca3 done                                                                             
.
.
.
Copying config a968a1c4d9 done                                                                             Writing manifest to image destination                                                                      
Storing signatures                                                                                           no space left on device                                                                                  
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/ansible/awx_task:10.0.0...                                         manifest unknown: manifest unknown                                                                       
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ansible/awx_task:10.0.0...                                         name unknown: Repo not found                                                                             
Trying to pull registry.centos.org/ansible/awx_task:10.0.0...                                                manifest unknown: manifest unknown                                                                       
Trying to pull quay.io/ansible/awx_task:10.0.0...                                                            unauthorized: access to the requested resource is not authorized                                         
Error: error pulling image "ansible/awx_task:10.0.0": unable to pull ansible/awx_task:10.0.0: 5 errors occ$
rred:                                                                                                      
        * Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:714266dcb362cddc2ed40b$
0a9b0a1c67ff0c61db52b141eef20b645a4f1252f": error creating layer with ID "b9d085448ef0405d2eaf94243ea273fa$
cac515cd798efe62a94aed39c7cba30": no space left on device                                                  
        * Error initializing source docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/ansible/awx_task:10.0.0: Error read
ing manifest 10.0.0 in registry.fedoraproject.org/ansible/awx_task: manifest unknown: manifest unknown
        * Error initializing source docker://registry.access.redhat.com/ansible/awx_task:10.0.0: Error read
ing manifest 10.0.0 in registry.access.redhat.com/ansible/awx_task: name unknown: Repo not found
        * Error initializing source docker://registry.centos.org/ansible/awx_task:10.0.0: Error reading man
ifest 10.0.0 in registry.centos.org/ansible/awx_task: manifest unknown: manifest unknown
        * Error initializing source docker://quay.io/ansible/awx_task:10.0.0: Error reading manifest 10.0.0
 in quay.io/ansible/awx_task: unauthorized: access to the requested resource is not authorized

```


**Describe the results you expected:**

Doing the same on my RHEL8 leads into 2.1GB image:

```
REPOSITORY                                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED        SIZE
docker.io/ansible/awx_task                 10.0.0   a968a1c4d9fd   2 weeks ago    2.1 GB
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I tried several times. In between the commands I ```sudo rm -rf .local/share/containers```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

comments.body: Could this be the pull was being stored on /tmp and this ran out of space?

Good idea. I forgot to add here that fedora-iot has a bit different directory layout, so pretty much everything is under /var, mounted from root LMV. It is the users ~/.local/share/containers that get's full.

Before the pull:
```
[ikke@fediot ~]$ df -h
Filesystem                    Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
devtmpfs                      3.9G     0  3.9G   0% /dev
tmpfs                         3.9G  168K  3.9G   1% /dev/shm
tmpfs                         3.9G  860K  3.9G   1% /run
/dev/mapper/fedora--iot-root   18G  1.7G   15G  10% /sysroot
/dev/vda2                     976M   55M  855M   6% /boot
/dev/vda1                     599M  8.3M  591M   2% /boot/efi
tmpfs                         796M   12K  796M   1% /run/user/1017
```

after the pull:

```
[ikke@fediot ~]$ df -h                                                                                            
Filesystem                    Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on                                                    
devtmpfs                      3.9G     0  3.9G   0% /dev                                                          
tmpfs                         3.9G  168K  3.9G   1% /dev/shm                                                      
tmpfs                         3.9G  852K  3.9G   1% /run                                                          
/dev/mapper/fedora--iot-root   18G   16G  856M  95% /sysroot                                                      
/dev/vda2                     976M   55M  855M   6% /boot                                                         
/dev/vda1                     599M  8.3M  591M   2% /boot/efi                                                     
tmpfs                         796M   12K  796M   1% /run/user/1017       
```

it does some weird mapping, as here in mount you can see the sysroot is on /var, and  many others too.

```
[ikke@fediot ~]$ mount
sysfs on /sys type sysfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,seclabel)
proc on /proc type proc (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime)
devtmpfs on /dev type devtmpfs (rw,nosuid,seclabel,size=4048204k,nr_inodes=1012051,mode=755)
securityfs on /sys/kernel/security type securityfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime)
tmpfs on /dev/shm type tmpfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,seclabel)
devpts on /dev/pts type devpts (rw,nosuid,noexec,relatime,seclabel,gid=5,mode=620,ptmxmode=000)
tmpfs on /run type tmpfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,seclabel,mode=755)
cgroup2 on /sys/fs/cgroup type cgroup2 (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,seclabel,nsdelegate)
pstore on /sys/fs/pstore type pstore (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,seclabel)
efivarfs on /sys/firmware/efi/efivars type efivarfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime)
none on /sys/fs/bpf type bpf (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,mode=700)
configfs on /sys/kernel/config type configfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime)
/dev/mapper/fedora--iot-root on /sysroot type ext4 (rw,relatime,seclabel,stripe=4)
/dev/mapper/fedora--iot-root on / type ext4 (rw,relatime,seclabel,stripe=4)
/dev/mapper/fedora--iot-root on /usr type ext4 (ro,relatime,seclabel,stripe=4)
selinuxfs on /sys/fs/selinux type selinuxfs (rw,relatime)
systemd-1 on /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc type autofs (rw,relatime,fd=31,pgrp=1,timeout=0,minproto=5,maxproto=5,direct,pipe_ino=16726)
mqueue on /dev/mqueue type mqueue (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,seclabel)
hugetlbfs on /dev/hugepages type hugetlbfs (rw,relatime,seclabel,pagesize=2M)
debugfs on /sys/kernel/debug type debugfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,seclabel)
/dev/mapper/fedora--iot-root on /var type ext4 (rw,relatime,seclabel,stripe=4)
/dev/vda2 on /boot type ext4 (rw,relatime,seclabel,stripe=4)
/dev/vda1 on /boot/efi type vfat (rw,relatime,fmask=0077,dmask=0077,codepage=437,iocharset=ascii,shortname=winnt,errors=remount-ro)
tmpfs on /run/user/1017 type tmpfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,relatime,seclabel,size=814644k,mode=700,uid=1017,gid=1017)
```

But eventually it is all the same, some layering black magic. I already did remove the image cruft from local dir, that's why it has 15G available here:

```
[ikke@fediot ~]$ df -h .local/share/containers/storage/mounts/
Filesystem                    Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/mapper/fedora--iot-root   18G  1.7G   15G  10% /var
```

and the env for the user, just in case:

```
[ikke@fediot ~]$ env     
SHELL=/bin/bash   
HISTCONTROL=ignoredups
HISTSIZE=1000
XMODIFIERS=@im=ibus
LC_MONETARY=C.UTF-8
PWD=/home/ikke
LOGNAME=ikke
XDG_SESSION_TYPE=tty
HOME=/home/ikke
LC_PAPER=C.UTF-8
LANG=en_US.UTF-8
XDG_SESSION_CLASS=user
SELINUX_ROLE_REQUESTED=
TERM=screen
LESSOPEN=||/usr/bin/lesspipe.sh %s
USER=ikke
NCURSES_NO_UTF8_ACS=1
TMUX_PANE=%0
SELINUX_USE_CURRENT_RANGE=
SHLVL=2
LC_MEASUREMENT=C.UTF-8
XDG_SESSION_ID=5
XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1017
LC_TIME=C.UTF-8
PATH=/home/ikke/.local/bin:/home/ikke/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/sbin
SELINUX_LEVEL_REQUESTED=
DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=unix:path=/run/user/1017/bus
MAIL=/var/spool/mail/ikke
SSH_TTY=/dev/pts/0
LC_NUMERIC=C.UTF-8
_=/usr/bin/env
```

That’s unexpected. Can you track down the exact new files/directories that are taking so much space, ideally down to the overlayfs layer or even the individual file?

---

The failing 714266dcb3… layer is 2560 bytes uncompressed, two directories and a symlink. It’s _possible_ that the process was a few kilobytes short of out of space and randomly hit that tiny layer, but it seems extremely unlikely.

@nalind any ideas?

The vfs driver builds each layer starting with a complete copy of its parent layer.  The image has 35 layers.  The first of those layers, decompressed, is a 234 megabyte blob that takes up around 246 megabytes of space once it's extracted.  The second layer, adding a 776 megabyte layer blob, results in a layer that takes up about 985 megabytes of space.  The third and fourth layer blobs are tiny, adding or overwriting a single 19 kilobyte file each, but the resulting layers take up 985 megabytes each.  I didn't keep going beyond the fourth layer, but I expect it continues to snowball after that.

I suspect that running `podman info --debug` on the RHEL 8 machine will confirm that it's using a different, more space-efficient driver, probably overlay.

Thanks for the good explanation. Yes, RHEL8 has "GraphDriverName: overlay". By the sounds of this, Fedora-IoT won't be the right choice for me then. Unless I could add another storage LV for containers with overlay set as the driver.
Why aren't you using overlay on Fedora-IoT?
If you install fuse-overlay then it will work in rootless mode.  Sorry I missed this.
YES! Thanks, that fixed it. The package name is actually fuse-overlayfs, and installing that took away the problem. Now it needs to be added to Fedora-IoT, as it's clearly critical component.
I close the issue, as this solved my problem.
It should be a recommended package for podman, is IOT being installed without recommended packages.  You should also make sure container-selinux is installed?

@rhatdan I created a ticket to fedora-iot bugzilla, where I learned the upstream (32) is already added with the overlayfs package. I run the latest official 31 version. And yes, it comes with container-selinux.

https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1824984
